10.gl: a slave (literal or figurative, involuntary or voluntary; frequently, therefore in a qualified sense of subjection or subserviency)

23.gl: a witness (literally (judicially) or figuratively (genitive case)); by analogy, a "martyr"

28.gl: decision (subjectively or objectively, for or against); by extension, a tribunal; by implication, justice (especially, divine law)

33.gl: decision (subjectively or objectively, for or against); by extension, a tribunal; by implication, justice (especially, divine law)

33.dv: probably from G939 (βάσις) (through the notion of a foundation of power);

36.gl: properly, royalty, i.e. (abstractly) rule, or (concretely) a realm (literally or figuratively)

37.dv: probably from G939 (βάσις) (through the notion of a foundation of power);

37.gl: to procreate (properly, of the father, but by extension of the mother); figuratively, to regenerate
37.kj: bear, beget, be born, bring forth, conceive, be delivered of, gender, make, spring

37.gl: a witness (literally (judicially) or figuratively (genitive case)); by analogy, a "martyr"

39.dv: probably from G939 (βάσις) (through the notion of a foundation of power);
